ObjectName.value: Hubble Photo Gallery of Jupiter's Galilean Satellites (1995-35-330)

Categories.value: Galilean moons|Photos of Jupiter system by Hubble|Hubble files uploaded by OptimusPrimeBot

ImageDescription.value: This is a Hubble Space Telescope "family portrait" of the four largest moons of Jupiter, first observed by the Italian scientist Galileo Galilei nearly four centuries ago. Located approximately one-half billion miles away, the moons are so small that, in visible light, they appear as fuzzy disks in the largest ground-based telescopes. Hubble can resolve surface details seen previously only by the Voyager spacecraft in the early 1980s. While the Voyagers provided close-up snapshots of the satellites, Hubble can now follow changes on the moons and reveal other characteristics at ultraviolet and near-infrared wavelengths.

Credit.value: Hubble Photo Gallery of Jupiter's Galilean Satellites

Artist.value: K. Noll (), J. Spencer (Lowell Observatory), and
